stories.title: Carlo Ancelotti holds talks over Italy job after Pep Guardiola discussions, Paolo Maldini says
stories.summary: The Italian Football Federation (FIGC) has spoken to Carlo Ancelotti in addition to Pep Guardiola regarding the national team’s vacant head coach role, technical director Paolo Maldini says. Giovanni Malago, the new head of the FIGC, said on July 21 that the federation had held talks with Guardiola over succeeding Gennaro Gattuso, who left the role in April following the country’s failure to qualify for the 2026 World Cup. Former Italy defender Maldini, 58, was appointed the FICG’s technical director this month and said on Thursday that conversations had been had not only with the former Manchester City manager, but also Brazil head coach Ancelotti.

stories.title: Garnacho seals loan move away from Chelsea in complicated deal as Blues close in on $105 million central defender
stories.summary: Chelsea have loaned Alejandro Garnacho to Aston Villa on a season-long loan with the option to buy. According to Sky Sports News, the move includes a “conditional obligation to buy” the Argentina international, who simply did not make the impact at Stamford Bridge that he had hoped after his move from Manchester United. With Chelsea needing to prune their squad without European football under Xabi Alonso, Garnacho was viewed as surplus to requirements. Watching your favourite European football has never been easier. For over 15 years, thousands of Australians have trusted Champions Travel for official tickets.

stories.title: Federal court ends oversight of Julia Tutwiler women’s prison in Wetumpka
stories.summary: Posted: Jul 23, 2026 5:32 PM CDT by WAKA Action 8 News Julia Tutwiler Prison for Women in Wetumpka – Photo from WAKA Action 8 News MONTGOMERY, Ala. (WAKA) – Attorney General Steve Marshall has announced the end of federal oversight of the Julia Tutwiler Prison for Women in Wetumpka. A final judgment has ended the case of U. S. v. State of Alabama and Alabama Department of Corrections, closing out more than a decade of federal oversight of the prison. U. S. District Judge Myron Thompson’s order dismissed the case and the consent decree, following an agreement between the U. S.

stories.title: Love Island SPOILER: 'Gameplay' accusations rock the villa as Maya Jama puts Islanders in the hot seat
stories.summary: By on 23rd July 2026 in By on 23rd July 2026 in The events of The Grafties have boiled over into another episode of Love Island , as accusations of 'gameplay' have unsettled the Islanders. In a first look at Thursday's episode, Priya questions Julia over her thoughts on her connection with Aidan. She asks: 'Do you think I’m genuinely with Aidan as a gameplay, to get to the final? ' Julia admits: 'I do think you’ve developed a connection. He would pick you over any new bombshell as that would guarantee the final. ' Priya and Yasmin, Love Island. Pic: ITV The conversation shifts to the wider dynamics in the Villa.

stories.title: The Showjumper Murder: Who was Katie Simpson and what happened to her?
stories.summary: The Showjumper Murder: Who was Katie Simpson and what happened to her? Katie Simpson was just 21 years when she died after suffering catastrophic injuries at a house in County Londonderry in August 2020. Police initially believed that the talented showjumper had attempted to take her own life after her brother-in-law and showjumping trainer Jonathan Creswell claimed he found her hanging from a stairwell. While speaking to authorities, he also claimed that Katie had sustained her injuries after a horse riding accident earlier that week.

stories.title: Miquita Oliver apologises to Fearne Cotton over past jibe
stories.summary: Miquita Oliver apologises to Fearne Cotton over 'awful' comment in the past Updated / Wednesday, 22 Jul 2026 18:15 Getty (L-R) Miquita Oliver and Fearne Cotton - Oliver was let go from her radio job in 2005 following an interview with NME where she was asked how she would improve Top of the Pops and replied that the programme should get rid of Cotton Presenter Miquita Oliver has apologised to Fearne Cotton after Oliver was sacked from BBC Radio 1 in 2005 for making an "awful" comment about her fellow broadcaster.

stories.title: Mohamed Salah is one step away from joining Besiktas.. New developments in the deal
stories.summary: Ankara, Turkey – Turkish club Besiktas is close to finalizing a deal to sign Egyptian star Mohamed Salah during the current summer transfer window, after negotiations witnessed new developments in recent hours. The negotiations had stalled due to a dispute over the commission of Ramy Abbas, Mohamed Salah’s agent, as Turkish reports indicated that he had requested a percentage of 35% of the total value of the contract, while Besiktas insisted that the commission should not exceed 15%.

stories.title: TV tonight: documentary reveals the shocking truth behind model family blogger Ruby Franke
stories.summary: The Devil in the Family: The Fall of Ruby Franke 9pm, BBC Two At its peak, Ruby Franke’s parenting YouTube channel was earning £100,000 a month. But, as this documentary series shows, her ostensibly model family was a house of cards. As her ex-husband puts it: “The devil knocked and we opened the door. ” Their downfall culminated in Franke’s arrest for child abuse in 2023 after her emaciated son escaped and summoned help. Thanks to the plentiful footage recorded by Franke herself, it’s easy to sense the roots of this horror. Phil Harrison The Chase Around the World 9pm, ITV1 The shrewd expansion of the Chase universe into a travel contest with a general knowledge quiz attached continues.

stories.title: Robin Williams defended Jumanji child stars Bradley Pierce and Kirsten Dunst on set
stories.summary: It has been almost 12 years since the death of Robin Williams shook the entertainment world – and this week would have been the actor’s 75th birthday. Since the actor’s death in 2014 , anecdotes about his many good deeds have continued to surface , including from Jumanji child star Bradley Pierce, who revealed how the actor stepped in to defend him from over-demanding producers. Alongside Kirsten Dunst, Pierce played one of the kids in the 1995 family film. In 2020, he recalled shooting a sequence in which the titular game leads to a fierce monsoon.

stories.title: Ukraine ‘priority’ for Ireland during EU presidency, Taoiseach says
stories.summary: The Irish premier has said it was “very important” to go to Ukraine early in Ireland’s EU presidency as a demonstration of solidarity. Micheál Martin was speaking to reporters in the country’s capital, Kyiv, on Thursday. Asked if there was a danger the EU’s focus would drift because of the war in the Middle East, he said “we have too many wars in the world”, adding that conflict has had “a very, very negative impact” there and on the wider world. The Taoiseach said it was “very, very important to come out here early” during Ireland’s six-month presidency of the Council of the European Union to make clear to Ukraine “they are a priority in terms of the Irish presidency”.

stories.title: Amy Winehouse Remembered on 15th Death Anniversary: Her 'Music Continues to Connect With People'
stories.summary: Legacy "Her voice, her songwriting, her honesty and her unique spirit remain as powerful today as ever," estate wrote in tribute Thursday Amy Winehouse ’s estate and family remembered the singer Thursday on the 15th anniversary of her death . “Fifteen years on, Amy’s music continues to connect with people around the world,” Winehouse’s official Instagram wrote in a tribute post Thursday. “Her voice, her songwriting, her honesty and her unique spirit remain as powerful today as ever. Thank you for keeping her memory alive through the music she loved so much. ” Winehouse died July 23, 2011 at the age of 27, with alcohol poisoning cited as her cause of “death by misadventure.

stories.title: Avatar: Seven Havens Officially Reveals Korra's Earthbender Successor With October 9 Premiere
stories.summary: Published Jul 23, 2026, 6:06 PM EDT Leo Reyna is an entertainment journalist and editor with extensive experience covering anime, manga, and gaming. Before joining CBR, he contributed to major entertainment outlets including Static Media and Ranker, where he developed a reputation for insightful industry coverage and a deep understanding of fan culture. At CBR, Leo reports on key developments within the anime industry—examining topics such as production challenges, studio practices, and creative communities—while also spotlighting the passion and artistry of fandoms worldwide.

stories.title: Highlights | Hull FC v Robins
stories.summary:
Watch all the highlights as Rivals Returns kicked off with a huge Hull Derby as Hull FC welcomed the Robins to MKM Stadium looking to bring to an end their disappointing run in the fixture.

Willie Peters men on the other hand were looking for a record eighth consecutive derby victory in the Betfred Super League.

Never miss the highlights by subscribing to the Betfred Super League YouTube channel and for more content, including full-length matches, classic games and features subscribe to SuperLeague+ today!

stories.title: Highlights FK Vojvodina - Ajax | Abdalla puts finishing touch on victory
stories.summary: Ajax have taken a strong advantage into the return leg of their UEFA Conference League tie against FK Vojvodina at the Johan Cruijff ArenA. The first leg in Serbia ended in a convincing 1-4 win for the Ajax side. Debutant Mohamed Abdalla scored Ajax's fourth goal of the evening, putting the finishing touch on an impressive victory. Date: 23. 07. 2026 Photographer: AFC Ajax Related articles In conversation with Míchel Sánchez about his philosophy, the squad and FK Vojvodina Ahead of Ajax's first competitive fixture of the season, away to FK Vojvodina, we sat down for an in-depth conversation with head coach Míchel Sánchez. How did he experienced his first few weeks at Ajax?

stories.title: Stuttgart divided over Ermedin Demirovic’s future amid interest from Leeds and others
stories.summary: The Sky Germany Transfer Update Show reports that Ermedin Demirovic’s future at VfB Stuttgart has become the subject of internal debate amid interest from Juventus, Fenerbahçe and Leeds United. Evidently, head coach Sebastian Hoeneß is open to selling the 28-year-old. Stuttgart’s administrative team nevertheless retain a different view. Stuttgart are presently planning with a three-striker rotation featuring Demirovic, Deniz Undav and Wolfsburg talent Dzenan Pejcinovic. The latter has already agreed personal terms, though the clubs have yet to finalize a transfer. Jeremy Arevalo would serve as a developmental option, while Jovan Milosevic remains free to leave.

stories.title: Alfredo Villordo earns CPA license at Lauterbach, Borschow & Co.
stories.summary: Alfredo Villordo, a tax supervisor at Lauterbach, Borschow & Co., has become the latest in a string of professionals to earn a Certified Public Accountant (CPA) license. This achievement underscores the firm's commitment to professional development and expertise in accounting services.

stories.title: 5-Part Dragon Series Was The Best Fantasy On TV Before Game Of Thrones
stories.summary: Published Jul 21, 2026, 2:00 PM EDT Cathal Gunning has been writing about movies, television, culture, and politics online and in print since 2017. He worked as a Senior Editor in Adbusters Media Foundation from 2018-2019 and wrote for WhatCulture in early 2020. He has been a Senior Features Writer for ScreenRant since 2020. Although one underrated adventure series from 2008 was once one of the best fantasy shows in TV history, the arrival of Game of Thrones knocked this BBC show from its throne fairly fast. Some TV shows are simply destined to change the medium’s history forever with their arrival.

stories.title: Pre-order EA SPORTS FC™ 27
stories.summary: Digital render of Kylian Mbappé along with a logo of EA SPORTS FC™ 27. is now the current item in the media gallery The World’s Game. Watch the full EA SPORTS FC™ 27 reveal on July 23. Subscribe to see more from EA SPORTS FC: http://bit. ly/pzw4VD Available on Sep 24, 2026 - 11 PM UTC On EA app starting from You are buying a license to access this digital content per the EA User Agreement . and Terms Of Sale apply. This game includes optional in-game purchases of virtual currency that can be used to acquire virtual in-game items including a random selection of virtual in-game items.

stories.title: Shaking Up the Emmys By Moving Five Categories Out of the Telecast Might Not Work, But at Least the TV Academy Is Trying Something
stories.summary: Richard Gadd, change your flight plans. The creator and star of HBO Max’s “Half Man” wound up with the series’ only Emmy nomination this year — via his supporting actor in a limited/anthology series or movie nod. But as of last week, that category will no longer be presented during NBC’s Emmy telecast on Sept. 14. Last Thursday, the TV Academy Board of Governors voted to move five categories to the earlier Emmy ceremonies (no longer dubbed the “Creative Arts Emmys ,” now they’re just “Emmys”): writing for a variety series, supporting actor and actress in a limited series or movie, directing for a limited series or movie and writing for a limited series or movie.

stories.title: King's Tardis entrance as Commonwealth Games begin
stories.summary: King Charles III emerges from a Tardis and a Loch Ness Monster takes the stage at the Commonwealth Games opening ceremony in Glasgow. One of the louder cheers came for the Falklands Islands – in the news recently following Argentina's victory over England in the football World Cup semi-finals – and another followed when the Samoa team emerged and performed a haka in front of the dignitaries in the Royal Box. But nothing compared to the noise which greeted the arrival of the Scotland team, who entered the arena last in their white shirts and kilts and lifted many in the capacity crowd from their seats.

stories.title: US health panel recommends easing restrictions on controversial peptides
stories.summary: A federal health advisory panel on Thursday narrowly voted 8-6, with one abstention, to ease restrictions on injectable peptides like BPC-157 and TB-500, despite safety concerns from government scientists. The non-binding recommendation could allow pharmacies to produce these compounds for wellness and cosmetic uses if the FDA adopts the advice. Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr., a vocal peptide advocate, has signaled plans to overturn existing FDA restrictions.

stories.title: US announces tariffs on dozens of trade partners over 'forced labour' imports
stories.summary: The US is imposing new tariffs of 10% to 12.5% on imports from about 60 trading partners, including the UK, EU, Canada, Japan and India, over forced labour concerns. The duties take effect Friday under President Trump’s expanded trade policy after a Supreme Court ruling blocked earlier tariffs. The move aims to pressure countries to strengthen labour protections and address trade distortions.

stories.title: Derry's European fate in the balance after narrow defeat in Croatia
stories.summary: Updated / Thursday, 23 Jul 2026 22:01 Derry face a 1-0 deficit in their home return leg Derry City's Europa Conference League tie against HNK Rijeka is still in the balance after the first leg, at a rain-soaked Stadion HNK. Croatian international Toni Fruk's second half goal was enough to separate the sides, but City boss Tiernan Lynch will take heart that his side weren't troubled for long periods and will hoping they can have another memorable night at the Ryan McBride Brandywell Stadium, for next Thursday night's second leg. The Candystripes produced a super professional first half and nullified the Croatia men's threat, while in the final 10 minutes they slowly grew into the contest.

stories.title: President Macron talks Modesto in meeting with George Lucas. What happened in Paris
stories.summary: Get the Eat. Sip. Play. newsletter in your inbox Available now: Sign up here for the best food, drink and entertainment coverage in Stanislaus County. Modesto native George Lucas received a prestigious award from French President Emmanuel Macron this month. Lucas was one of five in the movie industry honored during a ceremony July 15 in Paris, according to a story on Variety. com . Along with Lucas, actresses Jodie Foster and Sigourney Weaver and Illumination founder and CEO Chris Meledandri were presented with insignias of Knight of the Legion of Honor, the story said, and French filmmaker Claude Lelouch received the insignia of Commander of Legion of Merit.

stories.title: Hull KR inflict more derby misery on Hull FC
stories.summary: Out-of-form Super League champions Hull KR put a stop to their losing run as they battle back to beat city rivals Hull FC in a feisty derby. Betfred Super League Hull FC (12) 20 Tries: Batchelor, D Litten, Arthur Goals: Hardaker 4 Hull KR (12) 28 Tries: Amone, Mourgue, Broadbent, Burgess, Dezaria Goals: Mourgue 7 Out-of-form Super League champions Hull KR put a stop to their losing run as they battled back to beat city rivals Hull FC in a feisty derby.

stories.title: Anthony Joshua vs Kristian Prenga: Briton embraces comeback spotlight before fight in Saudi Arabia
stories.summary: Anthony Joshua is set to make his comeback against Albanian boxer Kristian Prenga in Saudi Arabia on Saturday, marking his first fight since a tragic car crash in Nigeria last December that claimed two of his friends. A victory could pave the way for a highly anticipated all-British heavyweight clash with Tyson Fury, contingent on Fury's own success in his upcoming fight. Joshua expressed his determination and focus, stating that he is ready to "conquer the universe" starting with this match.

stories.title: Icon penniless after $60m ‘betrayal’
stories.summary: Former world No. 1 tennis player Arantxa Sanchez Vicario has revealed new details after her $60m fortune “vanished”. The four-time grand slam champ is still living a nightmare after she was handed a suspended two-year prison sentence and ordered to pay back $11 million worth of debts. The Australian Open finalist has given a tell-all interview with a Spanish TV show where she made new accusations against her ex-husband — the person she blames for her financial downfall. The 54-year-old was married to entrepreneur Josep Santacana between 2008 and 2019. They have two children. The former teenage sensation has turned on her former partner — blaming him entirely for the family’s financial ruin.

stories.title: Will Smith Debuted a Shaved Head at the Monaco E1 Grand Prix with Prince Albert
stories.summary: Monaco has a long and storied history with racing sports, and it just added a anotherone to its roster. On July 18, the principality played host to the E1 Series Monaco Grand Prix, an all-electric boat racing series that’s the brainchild of Spanish entrepreneur Alejandro Agag. Though the event is only in its third season, it’s already attracted plenty of A-list attention, including the likes of Oscar-winner Will Smith. Smith showed up to the race with an eye-catching new look, sporting a completely shaved head.

stories.title: Trump Administration to Impose New Tariffs of Around 10% on Over 80 Nations
stories.summary: The administration announced new duties tied to countries’ imports of goods made with forced labor, replacing a global 10 percent tariff set to expire at midnight.

stories.title: Sturz in Geröllrinne: Deutsche stirbt am Fellhorn
stories.summary: A 63-year-old German tourist died in the Austrian Alps on Thursday after falling 60 meters down a scree slope near the Fellhorn summit. The woman, hiking with her husband on a marked trail in Kleinwalsertal, slipped while returning to the Söllereckbahn mountain station. German and Austrian rescue teams confirmed her death at the scene.

stories.title: Burnham to spend first day working from northern branch of No 10
stories.summary: Andy Burnham will begin his first day working at the northern branch of Downing Street, known as No 10 North, in Manchester. This initiative aims to decentralize power from Westminster, with up to 300 civil servants expected to join by the end of next year. Burnham will chair a meeting of the National Economic Council on Friday, marking a significant shift in how strategic decisions regarding devolution and local economic growth are made.

stories.title: What happened on July 22?
stories.summary: On July 22, a powerful earthquake struck near the coast of Morocco, registering a magnitude of 6.8 and resulting in significant damage and loss of life. The tremor was felt across several regions, prompting emergency services to respond swiftly to the affected areas. Authorities are now assessing the full extent of the destruction and coordinating relief efforts for those impacted by this natural disaster.

stories.title: ‘Drag Race’ stars threaten to ‘sue’ Nancy Mace over attack on drag queens
stories.summary: Republican Rep. Nancy Mace launched a new attack on drag queens this week, but the Drag Race divas are hitting back. The South Carolina conservative drew attention during a congressional hearing when she displayed photographs of Drag Race host RuPaul and stars Kandy Muse and Joey Jay as she argued that drag “sexualizes” children. In a subcommittee hearing Tuesday, examining the Trump administration’s assertion that the Smithsonian is insufficiently patriotic, Republican Rep. Tim Burchett said the institution had "become infected by a woke ideology. " Mace criticized the National Museum of American History for displaying a photo of drag legend and Emmy-winner RuPaul.

stories.title: India activist ends hunger strike after 26 days
stories.summary: Activist Sonam Wangchuk ended his 26-day hunger strike after India’s government guaranteed no legal action against protesters at Delhi’s Jantar Mantar. The move follows a violent police crackdown on Monday’s march to parliament that injured dozens. Youth-led group CJP, now a major dissent voice, vows to keep pressing for Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan’s resignation.

stories.title: Alexa Plus is getting an AI update to handle more complicated instructions
stories.summary: Amazon is rolling out an AI update for Alexa Plus that lets it control more smart home devices, including washing machines and vacuums, by understanding complex voice commands. The upgrade, now in preview, connects to brands like Bosch, Whirlpool, and iRobot, letting users say things like “deep clean this jersey” and have Alexa pick the right wash cycle. Amazon plans to expand these third-party integrations to make the assistant more useful in daily routines.

stories.title: Should Travelers Give Up on Summer in Western Europe Because of the Heat?
stories.summary: A favorite vacation option may be at risk because of rising temperatures. Even those sticking with their summer plans agree: More air conditioning would help.

stories.title: Google and Tesla shares plunge as AI spending rattles markets
stories.summary: As major tech companies race to capitalize on a new wave of AI technology, investors wonder when financial benefits will appear. Shares of Google and Tesla plunged on Thursday as investors were spooked by the ever-increasing amounts of money being spent on artificial intelligence (AI). Google's parent company Alphabet saw its share price drop by nearly 7%, while Elon Musk's electric vehicle-maker Tesla saw its stock fall 14. 5%. Both reported negative free cash flow - the money retained after paying for operations and investments - in financial results on Wednesday, alongside promises to spend billions more in the months and years to come.

stories.title: Intel Benefits From a New Shift in A.I. Spending
stories.summary: The Silicon Valley chipmaker’s revenue rose 25 percent in the latest quarter, its fastest growth in 15 years, as A.I. firms increasingly bought chips known as central processing units.

stories.title: Lawmakers push for AI 'kill switch' after OpenAI goes rogue
stories.summary: A new bill would let the US government order the shutdown of AI models that pose a public threat. US lawmakers want to give the government the ability to quickly order the turning off of artificial intelligence (AI) tools that may threaten the public. Congressman Ted Lieu, a Democrat, and Congressman Nathaniel Moran, a Republican, on Thursday introduced a bill named the AI Kill Switch Act. They did so in light of OpenAI's recent admission that its AI models went out of control in an "unprecedented" way and hacked into a major repository of computer coding information.

stories.title: A Peptide Showdown: F.D.A. Scientists May Clash With R.F.K.’s Agenda
stories.summary: In an all-day hearing, the agency’s scientists argued against allowing broad access to unproven drugs that the agency had banned three years ago.

stories.title: U.S. Withdraws Subpoenas Issued to New York Times Journalists
stories.summary: A federal judge raised questions about the government’s handling of the subpoenas, which The Times called an attempt to intimidate the free press.

stories.title: Did the World Cup Live Up to Tourism Expectations? Here Are the Winners and Losers.
stories.summary: Host cities, hotels, tourism boards and more had lofty expectations for the World Cup, but some struck gold, while some fizzled. Here’s the breakdown.

stories.title: Number of U.S. Troop Deaths in Iran War Appears Lower on Pentagon Website
stories.summary: Three military officials and a Pentagon spokesman gave conflicting explanations for why the number of Americans killed in the Iran war had changed.

stories.title: The Maserati MC12 legend revived in a GT2 Stradale Fuoriserie unveiled in Modena
stories.summary: Modena, July 23, 2026 – Maserati celebrates the latest creation from the Maserati Fuoriserie program with the unveiling of a special GT2 Stradale built to order for a prominent Taiwanese collector. Developed through a close collaboration between the customer, the Modena team, and the Centro Stile, this bespoke model pays tribute to the legendary MC12–one of the most iconic Maseratis in recent history– reinterpreting its spirit through an elaborate configuration that combines the Trident's racing heritage, stylistic refinement, and Italian craftsmanship. The spectacular GT2 Stradale “Tribute to MC12” was unveiled and handed over to its owner at Maserati headquarters in Modena.

stories.title: Microsoft responds to LG monitors installing McAfee ads on Windows
stories.summary: Microsoft has removed the LG Monitor App Installer from the Microsoft Store after reports it secretly installed McAfee ads on Windows without user consent. The app, which came preloaded on some high-end LG monitors, had broad system access and collected sensitive data for years. Microsoft says it is reviewing its policies for third-party peripheral apps.

stories.title: Murder of Thomas in Crépol: the court upholds the aggravating circumstance of racism – Interview
stories.summary: The investigating judge in Valence has reclassified the charges against the suspects in the murder of Thomas Perotto in Crépol. The magistrates have added an aggravating circumstance to the charges against the accused. This decision comes after lengthy debates that have characterized the investigation since its inception. A debated legal classification The aggravating circumstance cited relates to the fact that the victims were allegedly attacked because they were white and French citizens. This legal classification had been the subject of much debate since the beginning of the investigation. Thomas Perotto was killed in 2023 in an event that deeply shocked the public.

stories.title: BBC Inside Science
stories.summary: Will the US army benefit from testing its male soldiers for testosterone levels? Home News Sport Weather iPlayer Sounds Bitesize CBeebies CBBC Food Home News Sport Business Technology Health Culture Arts Travel Earth Audio Video Live Use BBC. com or the new BBC App to listen to BBC podcasts, Radio 4 and the World Service outside the UK. Find out how to listen to other BBC stations Episode details Radio 4 , · 23 Jul 2026 , · 28 mins Available for 35 days Secretary of Defence Pete Hegseth has announced that the US military will be screening military personnel aged 30 and older for testosterone deficiency.

stories.title: U.S. Military Presence in Jordan Makes It Iranian Target
stories.summary: Relying heavily on U.S. aid and protection, Jordan is unlikely to restrict the American military presence there, despite escalating Iranian airstrikes.

stories.title: Magnicity unveils reimagined euromast to boost rotterdams appeal for international travellers
stories.summary: Experiential 2 min Read Magnicity has reopened the renovated Euromast in Rotterdam following an extensive refurbishment. The transformed attraction introduces immersive storytelling, upgraded dining and enhanced observation experiences while targeting growing international markets, including India. The company aims to position the landmark as a key addition to premium leisure and MICE itineraries across the Netherlands. Magnicity has unveiled the newly renovated Euromast in Rotterdam, introducing a refreshed visitor experience that combines immersive attractions, upgraded hospitality and panoramic views of the Dutch city.

stories.title: FDA reports new outbreak of explosive diarrhea with 72 cases identified
stories.summary: The FDA reports 72 new cases of explosive diarrhea linked to a Taylor Farms lettuce recall affecting 27 states. The outbreak may involve iceberg lettuce sold at Walmart, Jack in the Box, and through distributors like Sysco and US Foods. Health officials are investigating but have not identified the source or locations of the cases.

stories.title: Look for NASA+ Now Streaming on Amazon Fire TV
stories.summary: NASA+ is now available on Amazon Fire TV, expanding free, ad-free streaming access for viewers. Fire TV customers can tune in via Alexa+ on compatible devices. Future coverage will include Artemis missions, lunar deliveries and the first crewed Moon landing under Artemis.

stories.title: Key Republican Senator Says He Will Support Erica Schwartz for C.D.C. Director
stories.summary: Senator Bill Cassidy, who is believed to hold the deciding vote in the Senate health committee for Dr. Erica Schwartz’s confirmation, had declined for days to say whether he would back her.

stories.title: The only way to stop petrol pump thieves | Brief letters
stories.summary: I was once visiting Massachusetts in the US and forgot to take my card into the gas station before filling up ( ‘A guy did a runner in a Jaguar’: the petrol theft scandal – and the garage owner struggling to stop it, 21 July ). After some loudspeaker remonstration, I took my card in, apologising that I’d forgotten as we don’t do that in the UK. The owner almost fell on the floor laughing. “How do you stop people stealing gas? ” he asked. “We don’t,” I replied. Further merriment ensued. Sadly, it’s time we did start not releasing pumps until we have cash or a card deposit on hand.

offbeat.title: Carlo Ancelotti holds talks over Italy job after Pep Guardiola discussions, Paolo Maldini says
offbeat.summary: The Italian Football Federation (FIGC) has spoken to Carlo Ancelotti in addition to Pep Guardiola regarding the national team’s vacant head coach role, technical director Paolo Maldini says. Giovanni Malago, the new head of the FIGC, said on July 21 that the federation had held talks with Guardiola over succeeding Gennaro Gattuso, who left the role in April following the country’s failure to qualify for the 2026 World Cup. Former Italy defender Maldini, 58, was appointed the FICG’s technical director this month and said on Thursday that conversations had been had not only with the former Manchester City manager, but also Brazil head coach Ancelotti.

offbeat.summary: The Showjumper Murder: Who was Katie Simpson and what happened to her? Katie Simpson was just 21 years when she died after suffering catastrophic injuries at a house in County Londonderry in August 2020. Police initially believed that the talented showjumper had attempted to take her own life after her brother-in-law and showjumping trainer Jonathan Creswell claimed he found her hanging from a stairwell. While speaking to authorities, he also claimed that Katie had sustained her injuries after a horse riding accident earlier that week.

offbeat.title: Miquita Oliver apologises to Fearne Cotton over past jibe
offbeat.summary: Miquita Oliver apologises to Fearne Cotton over 'awful' comment in the past Updated / Wednesday, 22 Jul 2026 18:15 Getty (L-R) Miquita Oliver and Fearne Cotton - Oliver was let go from her radio job in 2005 following an interview with NME where she was asked how she would improve Top of the Pops and replied that the programme should get rid of Cotton Presenter Miquita Oliver has apologised to Fearne Cotton after Oliver was sacked from BBC Radio 1 in 2005 for making an "awful" comment about her fellow broadcaster.

offbeat.title: Mohamed Salah is one step away from joining Besiktas.. New developments in the deal
offbeat.summary: Ankara, Turkey – Turkish club Besiktas is close to finalizing a deal to sign Egyptian star Mohamed Salah during the current summer transfer window, after negotiations witnessed new developments in recent hours. The negotiations had stalled due to a dispute over the commission of Ramy Abbas, Mohamed Salah’s agent, as Turkish reports indicated that he had requested a percentage of 35% of the total value of the contract, while Besiktas insisted that the commission should not exceed 15%.

offbeat.title: TV tonight: documentary reveals the shocking truth behind model family blogger Ruby Franke
offbeat.summary: The Devil in the Family: The Fall of Ruby Franke 9pm, BBC Two At its peak, Ruby Franke’s parenting YouTube channel was earning £100,000 a month. But, as this documentary series shows, her ostensibly model family was a house of cards. As her ex-husband puts it: “The devil knocked and we opened the door. ” Their downfall culminated in Franke’s arrest for child abuse in 2023 after her emaciated son escaped and summoned help. Thanks to the plentiful footage recorded by Franke herself, it’s easy to sense the roots of this horror. Phil Harrison The Chase Around the World 9pm, ITV1 The shrewd expansion of the Chase universe into a travel contest with a general knowledge quiz attached continues.

offbeat.title: Robin Williams defended Jumanji child stars Bradley Pierce and Kirsten Dunst on set
offbeat.summary: It has been almost 12 years since the death of Robin Williams shook the entertainment world – and this week would have been the actor’s 75th birthday. Since the actor’s death in 2014 , anecdotes about his many good deeds have continued to surface , including from Jumanji child star Bradley Pierce, who revealed how the actor stepped in to defend him from over-demanding producers. Alongside Kirsten Dunst, Pierce played one of the kids in the 1995 family film. In 2020, he recalled shooting a sequence in which the titular game leads to a fierce monsoon.

offbeat.title: Ukraine ‘priority’ for Ireland during EU presidency, Taoiseach says
offbeat.summary: The Irish premier has said it was “very important” to go to Ukraine early in Ireland’s EU presidency as a demonstration of solidarity. Micheál Martin was speaking to reporters in the country’s capital, Kyiv, on Thursday. Asked if there was a danger the EU’s focus would drift because of the war in the Middle East, he said “we have too many wars in the world”, adding that conflict has had “a very, very negative impact” there and on the wider world. The Taoiseach said it was “very, very important to come out here early” during Ireland’s six-month presidency of the Council of the European Union to make clear to Ukraine “they are a priority in terms of the Irish presidency”.

offbeat.title: Amy Winehouse Remembered on 15th Death Anniversary: Her 'Music Continues to Connect With People'
offbeat.summary: Legacy "Her voice, her songwriting, her honesty and her unique spirit remain as powerful today as ever," estate wrote in tribute Thursday Amy Winehouse ’s estate and family remembered the singer Thursday on the 15th anniversary of her death . “Fifteen years on, Amy’s music continues to connect with people around the world,” Winehouse’s official Instagram wrote in a tribute post Thursday. “Her voice, her songwriting, her honesty and her unique spirit remain as powerful today as ever. Thank you for keeping her memory alive through the music she loved so much. ” Winehouse died July 23, 2011 at the age of 27, with alcohol poisoning cited as her cause of “death by misadventure.

offbeat.title: Avatar: Seven Havens Officially Reveals Korra's Earthbender Successor With October 9 Premiere
offbeat.summary: Published Jul 23, 2026, 6:06 PM EDT Leo Reyna is an entertainment journalist and editor with extensive experience covering anime, manga, and gaming. Before joining CBR, he contributed to major entertainment outlets including Static Media and Ranker, where he developed a reputation for insightful industry coverage and a deep understanding of fan culture. At CBR, Leo reports on key developments within the anime industry—examining topics such as production challenges, studio practices, and creative communities—while also spotlighting the passion and artistry of fandoms worldwide.

offbeat.title: Highlights | Hull FC v Robins
offbeat.summary:
Watch all the highlights as Rivals Returns kicked off with a huge Hull Derby as Hull FC welcomed the Robins to MKM Stadium looking to bring to an end their disappointing run in the fixture.

Willie Peters men on the other hand were looking for a record eighth consecutive derby victory in the Betfred Super League.

Never miss the highlights by subscribing to the Betfred Super League YouTube channel and for more content, including full-length matches, classic games and features subscribe to SuperLeague+ today!

offbeat.title: Highlights FK Vojvodina - Ajax | Abdalla puts finishing touch on victory
offbeat.summary: Ajax have taken a strong advantage into the return leg of their UEFA Conference League tie against FK Vojvodina at the Johan Cruijff ArenA. The first leg in Serbia ended in a convincing 1-4 win for the Ajax side. Debutant Mohamed Abdalla scored Ajax's fourth goal of the evening, putting the finishing touch on an impressive victory. Date: 23. 07. 2026 Photographer: AFC Ajax Related articles In conversation with Míchel Sánchez about his philosophy, the squad and FK Vojvodina Ahead of Ajax's first competitive fixture of the season, away to FK Vojvodina, we sat down for an in-depth conversation with head coach Míchel Sánchez. How did he experienced his first few weeks at Ajax?

offbeat.title: Stuttgart divided over Ermedin Demirovic’s future amid interest from Leeds and others
offbeat.summary: The Sky Germany Transfer Update Show reports that Ermedin Demirovic’s future at VfB Stuttgart has become the subject of internal debate amid interest from Juventus, Fenerbahçe and Leeds United. Evidently, head coach Sebastian Hoeneß is open to selling the 28-year-old. Stuttgart’s administrative team nevertheless retain a different view. Stuttgart are presently planning with a three-striker rotation featuring Demirovic, Deniz Undav and Wolfsburg talent Dzenan Pejcinovic. The latter has already agreed personal terms, though the clubs have yet to finalize a transfer. Jeremy Arevalo would serve as a developmental option, while Jovan Milosevic remains free to leave.

offbeat.title: Alfredo Villordo earns CPA license at Lauterbach, Borschow & Co.
offbeat.summary: Alfredo Villordo, a tax supervisor at Lauterbach, Borschow & Co., has become the latest in a string of professionals to earn a Certified Public Accountant (CPA) license. This achievement underscores the firm's commitment to professional development and expertise in accounting services.

offbeat.title: 5-Part Dragon Series Was The Best Fantasy On TV Before Game Of Thrones
offbeat.summary: Published Jul 21, 2026, 2:00 PM EDT Cathal Gunning has been writing about movies, television, culture, and politics online and in print since 2017. He worked as a Senior Editor in Adbusters Media Foundation from 2018-2019 and wrote for WhatCulture in early 2020. He has been a Senior Features Writer for ScreenRant since 2020. Although one underrated adventure series from 2008 was once one of the best fantasy shows in TV history, the arrival of Game of Thrones knocked this BBC show from its throne fairly fast. Some TV shows are simply destined to change the medium’s history forever with their arrival.

offbeat.title: Pre-order EA SPORTS FC™ 27
offbeat.summary: Digital render of Kylian Mbappé along with a logo of EA SPORTS FC™ 27. is now the current item in the media gallery The World’s Game. Watch the full EA SPORTS FC™ 27 reveal on July 23. Subscribe to see more from EA SPORTS FC: http://bit. ly/pzw4VD Available on Sep 24, 2026 - 11 PM UTC On EA app starting from You are buying a license to access this digital content per the EA User Agreement . and Terms Of Sale apply. This game includes optional in-game purchases of virtual currency that can be used to acquire virtual in-game items including a random selection of virtual in-game items.

offbeat.title: Derry's European fate in the balance after narrow defeat in Croatia
offbeat.summary: Updated / Thursday, 23 Jul 2026 22:01 Derry face a 1-0 deficit in their home return leg Derry City's Europa Conference League tie against HNK Rijeka is still in the balance after the first leg, at a rain-soaked Stadion HNK. Croatian international Toni Fruk's second half goal was enough to separate the sides, but City boss Tiernan Lynch will take heart that his side weren't troubled for long periods and will hoping they can have another memorable night at the Ryan McBride Brandywell Stadium, for next Thursday night's second leg. The Candystripes produced a super professional first half and nullified the Croatia men's threat, while in the final 10 minutes they slowly grew into the contest.

offbeat.title: President Macron talks Modesto in meeting with George Lucas. What happened in Paris
offbeat.summary: Get the Eat. Sip. Play. newsletter in your inbox Available now: Sign up here for the best food, drink and entertainment coverage in Stanislaus County. Modesto native George Lucas received a prestigious award from French President Emmanuel Macron this month. Lucas was one of five in the movie industry honored during a ceremony July 15 in Paris, according to a story on Variety. com . Along with Lucas, actresses Jodie Foster and Sigourney Weaver and Illumination founder and CEO Chris Meledandri were presented with insignias of Knight of the Legion of Honor, the story said, and French filmmaker Claude Lelouch received the insignia of Commander of Legion of Merit.

offbeat.title: Anthony Joshua vs Kristian Prenga: Briton embraces comeback spotlight before fight in Saudi Arabia
offbeat.summary: Anthony Joshua is set to make his comeback against Albanian boxer Kristian Prenga in Saudi Arabia on Saturday, marking his first fight since a tragic car crash in Nigeria last December that claimed two of his friends. A victory could pave the way for a highly anticipated all-British heavyweight clash with Tyson Fury, contingent on Fury's own success in his upcoming fight. Joshua expressed his determination and focus, stating that he is ready to "conquer the universe" starting with this match.

offbeat.title: Icon penniless after $60m ‘betrayal’
offbeat.summary: Former world No. 1 tennis player Arantxa Sanchez Vicario has revealed new details after her $60m fortune “vanished”. The four-time grand slam champ is still living a nightmare after she was handed a suspended two-year prison sentence and ordered to pay back $11 million worth of debts. The Australian Open finalist has given a tell-all interview with a Spanish TV show where she made new accusations against her ex-husband — the person she blames for her financial downfall. The 54-year-old was married to entrepreneur Josep Santacana between 2008 and 2019. They have two children. The former teenage sensation has turned on her former partner — blaming him entirely for the family’s financial ruin.

offbeat.title: Will Smith Debuted a Shaved Head at the Monaco E1 Grand Prix with Prince Albert
offbeat.summary: Monaco has a long and storied history with racing sports, and it just added a anotherone to its roster. On July 18, the principality played host to the E1 Series Monaco Grand Prix, an all-electric boat racing series that’s the brainchild of Spanish entrepreneur Alejandro Agag. Though the event is only in its third season, it’s already attracted plenty of A-list attention, including the likes of Oscar-winner Will Smith. Smith showed up to the race with an eye-catching new look, sporting a completely shaved head.

offbeat.title: Sturz in Geröllrinne: Deutsche stirbt am Fellhorn
offbeat.summary: A 63-year-old German tourist died in the Austrian Alps on Thursday after falling 60 meters down a scree slope near the Fellhorn summit. The woman, hiking with her husband on a marked trail in Kleinwalsertal, slipped while returning to the Söllereckbahn mountain station. German and Austrian rescue teams confirmed her death at the scene.

offbeat.title: Burnham to spend first day working from northern branch of No 10
offbeat.summary: Andy Burnham will begin his first day working at the northern branch of Downing Street, known as No 10 North, in Manchester. This initiative aims to decentralize power from Westminster, with up to 300 civil servants expected to join by the end of next year. Burnham will chair a meeting of the National Economic Council on Friday, marking a significant shift in how strategic decisions regarding devolution and local economic growth are made.

offbeat.title: What happened on July 22?
offbeat.summary: On July 22, a powerful earthquake struck near the coast of Morocco, registering a magnitude of 6.8 and resulting in significant damage and loss of life. The tremor was felt across several regions, prompting emergency services to respond swiftly to the affected areas. Authorities are now assessing the full extent of the destruction and coordinating relief efforts for those impacted by this natural disaster.

offbeat.title: ‘Drag Race’ stars threaten to ‘sue’ Nancy Mace over attack on drag queens
offbeat.summary: Republican Rep. Nancy Mace launched a new attack on drag queens this week, but the Drag Race divas are hitting back. The South Carolina conservative drew attention during a congressional hearing when she displayed photographs of Drag Race host RuPaul and stars Kandy Muse and Joey Jay as she argued that drag “sexualizes” children. In a subcommittee hearing Tuesday, examining the Trump administration’s assertion that the Smithsonian is insufficiently patriotic, Republican Rep. Tim Burchett said the institution had "become infected by a woke ideology. " Mace criticized the National Museum of American History for displaying a photo of drag legend and Emmy-winner RuPaul.

offbeat.title: Google and Tesla shares plunge as AI spending rattles markets
offbeat.summary: As major tech companies race to capitalize on a new wave of AI technology, investors wonder when financial benefits will appear. Shares of Google and Tesla plunged on Thursday as investors were spooked by the ever-increasing amounts of money being spent on artificial intelligence (AI). Google's parent company Alphabet saw its share price drop by nearly 7%, while Elon Musk's electric vehicle-maker Tesla saw its stock fall 14. 5%. Both reported negative free cash flow - the money retained after paying for operations and investments - in financial results on Wednesday, alongside promises to spend billions more in the months and years to come.

offbeat.title: The Maserati MC12 legend revived in a GT2 Stradale Fuoriserie unveiled in Modena
offbeat.summary: Modena, July 23, 2026 – Maserati celebrates the latest creation from the Maserati Fuoriserie program with the unveiling of a special GT2 Stradale built to order for a prominent Taiwanese collector. Developed through a close collaboration between the customer, the Modena team, and the Centro Stile, this bespoke model pays tribute to the legendary MC12–one of the most iconic Maseratis in recent history– reinterpreting its spirit through an elaborate configuration that combines the Trident's racing heritage, stylistic refinement, and Italian craftsmanship. The spectacular GT2 Stradale “Tribute to MC12” was unveiled and handed over to its owner at Maserati headquarters in Modena.

offbeat.title: Microsoft responds to LG monitors installing McAfee ads on Windows
offbeat.summary: Microsoft has removed the LG Monitor App Installer from the Microsoft Store after reports it secretly installed McAfee ads on Windows without user consent. The app, which came preloaded on some high-end LG monitors, had broad system access and collected sensitive data for years. Microsoft says it is reviewing its policies for third-party peripheral apps.

offbeat.title: Murder of Thomas in Crépol: the court upholds the aggravating circumstance of racism – Interview
offbeat.summary: The investigating judge in Valence has reclassified the charges against the suspects in the murder of Thomas Perotto in Crépol. The magistrates have added an aggravating circumstance to the charges against the accused. This decision comes after lengthy debates that have characterized the investigation since its inception. A debated legal classification The aggravating circumstance cited relates to the fact that the victims were allegedly attacked because they were white and French citizens. This legal classification had been the subject of much debate since the beginning of the investigation. Thomas Perotto was killed in 2023 in an event that deeply shocked the public.

offbeat.title: Magnicity unveils reimagined euromast to boost rotterdams appeal for international travellers
offbeat.summary: Experiential 2 min Read Magnicity has reopened the renovated Euromast in Rotterdam following an extensive refurbishment. The transformed attraction introduces immersive storytelling, upgraded dining and enhanced observation experiences while targeting growing international markets, including India. The company aims to position the landmark as a key addition to premium leisure and MICE itineraries across the Netherlands. Magnicity has unveiled the newly renovated Euromast in Rotterdam, introducing a refreshed visitor experience that combines immersive attractions, upgraded hospitality and panoramic views of the Dutch city.

offbeat.title: FDA reports new outbreak of explosive diarrhea with 72 cases identified
offbeat.summary: The FDA reports 72 new cases of explosive diarrhea linked to a Taylor Farms lettuce recall affecting 27 states. The outbreak may involve iceberg lettuce sold at Walmart, Jack in the Box, and through distributors like Sysco and US Foods. Health officials are investigating but have not identified the source or locations of the cases.

entertainment.title: Shaking Up the Emmys By Moving Five Categories Out of the Telecast Might Not Work, But at Least the TV Academy Is Trying Something
entertainment.summary: Richard Gadd, change your flight plans. The creator and star of HBO Max’s “Half Man” wound up with the series’ only Emmy nomination this year — via his supporting actor in a limited/anthology series or movie nod. But as of last week, that category will no longer be presented during NBC’s Emmy telecast on Sept. 14. Last Thursday, the TV Academy Board of Governors voted to move five categories to the earlier Emmy ceremonies (no longer dubbed the “Creative Arts Emmys ,” now they’re just “Emmys”): writing for a variety series, supporting actor and actress in a limited series or movie, directing for a limited series or movie and writing for a limited series or movie.

sports.title: King's Tardis entrance as Commonwealth Games begin
sports.summary: King Charles III emerges from a Tardis and a Loch Ness Monster takes the stage at the Commonwealth Games opening ceremony in Glasgow. One of the louder cheers came for the Falklands Islands – in the news recently following Argentina's victory over England in the football World Cup semi-finals – and another followed when the Samoa team emerged and performed a haka in front of the dignitaries in the Royal Box. But nothing compared to the noise which greeted the arrival of the Scotland team, who entered the arena last in their white shirts and kilts and lifted many in the capacity crowd from their seats.

sports.title: Hull KR inflict more derby misery on Hull FC
sports.summary: Out-of-form Super League champions Hull KR put a stop to their losing run as they battle back to beat city rivals Hull FC in a feisty derby. Betfred Super League Hull FC (12) 20 Tries: Batchelor, D Litten, Arthur Goals: Hardaker 4 Hull KR (12) 28 Tries: Amone, Mourgue, Broadbent, Burgess, Dezaria Goals: Mourgue 7 Out-of-form Super League champions Hull KR put a stop to their losing run as they battled back to beat city rivals Hull FC in a feisty derby.

health.title: US health panel recommends easing restrictions on controversial peptides
health.summary: A federal health advisory panel on Thursday narrowly voted 8-6, with one abstention, to ease restrictions on injectable peptides like BPC-157 and TB-500, despite safety concerns from government scientists. The non-binding recommendation could allow pharmacies to produce these compounds for wellness and cosmetic uses if the FDA adopts the advice. Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr., a vocal peptide advocate, has signaled plans to overturn existing FDA restrictions.

health.title: Key Republican Senator Says He Will Support Erica Schwartz for C.D.C. Director
health.summary: Senator Bill Cassidy, who is believed to hold the deciding vote in the Senate health committee for Dr. Erica Schwartz’s confirmation, had declined for days to say whether he would back her.

health.title: The only way to stop petrol pump thieves | Brief letters
health.summary: I was once visiting Massachusetts in the US and forgot to take my card into the gas station before filling up ( ‘A guy did a runner in a Jaguar’: the petrol theft scandal – and the garage owner struggling to stop it, 21 July ). After some loudspeaker remonstration, I took my card in, apologising that I’d forgotten as we don’t do that in the UK. The owner almost fell on the floor laughing. “How do you stop people stealing gas? ” he asked. “We don’t,” I replied. Further merriment ensued. Sadly, it’s time we did start not releasing pumps until we have cash or a card deposit on hand.

crisis.title: US announces tariffs on dozens of trade partners over 'forced labour' imports
crisis.summary: The US is imposing new tariffs of 10% to 12.5% on imports from about 60 trading partners, including the UK, EU, Canada, Japan and India, over forced labour concerns. The duties take effect Friday under President Trump’s expanded trade policy after a Supreme Court ruling blocked earlier tariffs. The move aims to pressure countries to strengthen labour protections and address trade distortions.

crisis.title: Trump Administration to Impose New Tariffs of Around 10% on Over 80 Nations
crisis.summary: The administration announced new duties tied to countries’ imports of goods made with forced labor, replacing a global 10 percent tariff set to expire at midnight.

crisis.title: Did the World Cup Live Up to Tourism Expectations? Here Are the Winners and Losers.
crisis.summary: Host cities, hotels, tourism boards and more had lofty expectations for the World Cup, but some struck gold, while some fizzled. Here’s the breakdown.

breaking.title: India activist ends hunger strike after 26 days
breaking.summary: Activist Sonam Wangchuk ended his 26-day hunger strike after India’s government guaranteed no legal action against protesters at Delhi’s Jantar Mantar. The move follows a violent police crackdown on Monday’s march to parliament that injured dozens. Youth-led group CJP, now a major dissent voice, vows to keep pressing for Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan’s resignation.

breaking.title: Should Travelers Give Up on Summer in Western Europe Because of the Heat?
breaking.summary: A favorite vacation option may be at risk because of rising temperatures. Even those sticking with their summer plans agree: More air conditioning would help.

breaking.title: U.S. Military Presence in Jordan Makes It Iranian Target
breaking.summary: Relying heavily on U.S. aid and protection, Jordan is unlikely to restrict the American military presence there, despite escalating Iranian airstrikes.

tech.title: Alexa Plus is getting an AI update to handle more complicated instructions
tech.summary: Amazon is rolling out an AI update for Alexa Plus that lets it control more smart home devices, including washing machines and vacuums, by understanding complex voice commands. The upgrade, now in preview, connects to brands like Bosch, Whirlpool, and iRobot, letting users say things like “deep clean this jersey” and have Alexa pick the right wash cycle. Amazon plans to expand these third-party integrations to make the assistant more useful in daily routines.

tech.title: Intel Benefits From a New Shift in A.I. Spending
tech.summary: The Silicon Valley chipmaker’s revenue rose 25 percent in the latest quarter, its fastest growth in 15 years, as A.I. firms increasingly bought chips known as central processing units.

tech.title: Lawmakers push for AI 'kill switch' after OpenAI goes rogue
tech.summary: A new bill would let the US government order the shutdown of AI models that pose a public threat. US lawmakers want to give the government the ability to quickly order the turning off of artificial intelligence (AI) tools that may threaten the public. Congressman Ted Lieu, a Democrat, and Congressman Nathaniel Moran, a Republican, on Thursday introduced a bill named the AI Kill Switch Act. They did so in light of OpenAI's recent admission that its AI models went out of control in an "unprecedented" way and hacked into a major repository of computer coding information.

politics.title: A Peptide Showdown: F.D.A. Scientists May Clash With R.F.K.’s Agenda
politics.summary: In an all-day hearing, the agency’s scientists argued against allowing broad access to unproven drugs that the agency had banned three years ago.

politics.title: U.S. Withdraws Subpoenas Issued to New York Times Journalists
politics.summary: A federal judge raised questions about the government’s handling of the subpoenas, which The Times called an attempt to intimidate the free press.

politics.title: Number of U.S. Troop Deaths in Iran War Appears Lower on Pentagon Website
politics.summary: Three military officials and a Pentagon spokesman gave conflicting explanations for why the number of Americans killed in the Iran war had changed.

facts.title: BBC Inside Science
facts.summary: Will the US army benefit from testing its male soldiers for testosterone levels? Home News Sport Weather iPlayer Sounds Bitesize CBeebies CBBC Food Home News Sport Business Technology Health Culture Arts Travel Earth Audio Video Live Use BBC. com or the new BBC App to listen to BBC podcasts, Radio 4 and the World Service outside the UK. Find out how to listen to other BBC stations Episode details Radio 4 , · 23 Jul 2026 , · 28 mins Available for 35 days Secretary of Defence Pete Hegseth has announced that the US military will be screening military personnel aged 30 and older for testosterone deficiency.

facts.title: Look for NASA+ Now Streaming on Amazon Fire TV
facts.summary: NASA+ is now available on Amazon Fire TV, expanding free, ad-free streaming access for viewers. Fire TV customers can tune in via Alexa+ on compatible devices. Future coverage will include Artemis missions, lunar deliveries and the first crewed Moon landing under Artemis.
